title: Camera Feed Loss and Recording Gap Detection
dataSources: Poll your facility Video Management System REST endpoints through REST API Modular Input (Splunkbase app ID 1747) or deliver equivalent JSON probes via HTTP Event Collector `/services/collector/event`, landing telemetry on `index=physical` as `sourcetype="vms:stream_health"` with Splunk-parsed fields `fps`, `keyframes`, `recording_continuity`, `recording_gap_sec`, `stream_state`, `camera_id`, and `site`; align poll intervals (commonly five minutes, tighter during commissioning) with vendor rate limits.
spl:
index=physical sourcetype="vms:stream_health"
| where fps=0 OR recording_gap_sec > 60 OR stream_state="lost"
| stats latest(recording_gap_sec) as gap by camera_id, site
| sort -gap
description: Surfaces cameras whose live RTSP or HLS preview path reports zero FPS, `stream_state=lost`, or a sustained NVR-side `recording_gap_sec`, even when upstream ICMP or lightweight device-health pings still show the encoder reachable.
value: Prevents silent loss of CCTV evidence across data halls, perimeter lanes, and cage lines: operators can dispatch facilities or physical-security responders before blind intervals undermine incident reconstruction, visitor audits, or insurer and governance expectations for continuous recording.
implementation: Normalize each camera stream into `vms:stream_health` samples, retain FPS, keyframe cadence, recorder continuity counters, and explicit gap seconds from the VMS API or probe tier, then schedule the bundled SPL every five minutes with suppression keyed by `camera_id` and maintenance tags.
visualization: Primary Splunk table sorted by `recording_gap_sec` with conditional formatting for stream loss; paired timeline chart plotting `fps` and `recording_gap_sec` per `camera_id`; optional choropleth or SVG floor-plan overlay keyed by `site` for guard dispatch.

references.title: REST API Modular Input on Splunkbase (1747)
Splunk Docs — Configure alerts in Splunk Web
Splunk Docs — Send HTTP Event Collector data to Splunk Enterprise

knownFalsePositives:
• **Planned VMS or camera firmware window** — the Management Server UI shows an active upgrade task while `stream_state` flips transiently; suppress alerts using a CSV lookup keyed by `camera_id` with `maintenance=true` sourced from your approved change-management calendar export.
• **LAN QoS micro-bursts on the viewing VLAN** — RTSP preview drops frames but recorder continuity stays green; distinguish by requiring both `fps=0` and `recording_gap_sec` above policy before paging.
• **Night-mode aggressive GOP lengthening** — `keyframes` sparse yet `recording_continuity` counters increment normally; broaden the threshold using vendor-documented idle FPS instead of firing on preview FPS alone.
• **Archiver migration rewiring storage paths** — brief `recording_gap_sec` spikes during LUN failover; correlate with vendor backup/archiver job IDs present in companion logs and auto-suppress under ten minutes when tagged.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install REST API Modular Input (Splunkbase 1747) on a Heavy Forwarder that can reach the HTTPS management plane of your VMS, or approve an internal collector that forwards compact JSON through HEC without embedding secrets in dashboards.
• Map vendor responses into shared keys (`fps`, `keyframes`, `recording_continuity`, `recording_gap_sec`, `stream_state`, `camera_id`, `site`) before routing to `index=physical` / `sourcetype="vms:stream_health"`; document the exact REST paths (for example `/api/streams/status` or vendor-published equivalents) in the facility integration runbook.
• Grant operators a dedicated RBAC role such as `physical_security_analyst` read-only access to `index=physical`, and reserve `edit_alert_actions` for the automation service account.
• Follow docs/implementation-guide.md for standard `inputs.conf` placement, credential storage, and upgrade sequencing.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• Create modular-input stanzas that issue timed HTTPS GET/POST calls every five minutes (`cron` example `*/5 * * * *`) during steady state and every one to two minutes while new racks are commissioned.
• Stamp `_time` from the appliance-reported observation time inside the payload so gaps reflect recorder truth, not Splunk ingestion lag alone.
• If probes cannot hit the VMS directly, relay stream-health metrics through an approved intermediary that preserves the same sourcetype and field names.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Execute the UC SPL (saved as report first) across a `-15m` rolling window aligned to the modular-input cadence, then promote to a scheduled alert with digest mode enabled so multiple degraded cameras batch into one notification.

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="vms:stream_health"
| where fps=0 OR recording_gap_sec > 60 OR stream_state="lost"
| stats latest(recording_gap_sec) as gap by camera_id, site
| sort -gap
```

Understanding this SPL
• The base search pins `index=physical` and `sourcetype="vms:stream_health"`, matching the normalized telemetry described under Data sources.
• The `where` clause isolates true streaming/recording failures from benign idle scenes by combining FPS collapse, explicit gap seconds beyond sixty, or vendor-declared `stream_state="lost"`.
• `stats` rolls up the worst `recording_gap_sec` per `camera_id` and `site` so dashboards highlight entire aisles or halls rather than duplicate polling noise.
• `sort -gap` surfaces the longest outages first for incident bridges.

Step 3 — Validate
• Compare Splunk rows against the VMS operator console for the same `camera_id` interval and capture screenshots for evidence packs.
• Replay anonymized JSON fixtures on a dev indexer to confirm field spelling (`recording_gap_sec` vs alternate vendor labels) before promoting to production inputs.

Step 4 — Operationalize and troubleshoot
• Publish a Dashboard Studio or Classic XML panel pairing the alert table with a timeline chart keyed by `_time` and `camera_id`.
• If alerts spike during planned maintenance, temporarily narrow the search with Splunk `lookup` masks sourced from the change-management calendar, and document roll-forward steps when inputs stall (firewall rule, TLS certificate expiry, throttled API tokens).
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.

controlTest.positiveScenario: On a sandbox indexer, ingest labeled `vms:stream_health` events for camera_id LAB-AISLE-02 with fps=0, stream_state=lost, recording_gap_sec=125, and site=SJC-DC1 inside the scheduled five-minute window; run the saved search and verify it returns the row and triggers the configured alert action.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Replay healthy telemetry for the same camera_id with fps=30, stream_state=live, recording_gap_sec=4, steady keyframes, and unchanged site metadata across two polling intervals; confirm the search returns zero qualifying rows and no alert fires.

evidence: Export the scheduled search job inspector plus a CSV of triggering rows (`camera_id`, `site`, `recording_gap_sec`, `_time`) and attach the matching VMS operator-console screenshot for auditor review.
exclusions: Exclude sandbox streams, unmanned staging cages, or drone pilot kits mapped via `site=sandbox-*`; dedicate alternate indexes for retail demo feeds so they never inherit production alerting thresholds.
equipmentModels: VMS API with stream health probes (RTSP/HLS monitoring context)
